review_text: Keri Dennis, one of the staff who returns calls, is rude and has zero compassion for clients and what they are going through. Complete attitude every call. I do not recommend this firm.

They have a review screening for checking to see what cases are easy wins and which require them to actually work. I was turned down based on a fraction of the total information. In my opinion this law firm cares about money and not their client. I would use caution when doing business here.

Over two years and still waiting!!! I have not received any information about my case. Whenever I call I get attitude. They have no answers for why it is taking this long. They have no knowledge about anything when I call to ask about my case. This has been 2+ years and still have not heard anything.
Waste of time. Had me on hold 20 minutes to tell me they couldn’t take my case, without even hearing what it was about.

Contacted Hoglund to help with disability case. They said they would. Took all my info and had me sign papers to retain them. I keep calling them to talk to my agent about my case. So far my assigned agent will never return my calls after several attempts.

Everyone involved with this law firm could use some sensitivity training. They have no idea how to deal with a client that has anxiety and brain issues that affect their memory and thought process. I will probably need another lawyer to get me out of my contract with them now since I no longer want to work with them. I didn't even get through 5 minutes of the intake process without getting completely frustrated and was nearly in tears due to the guy's lack of consideration for my issues that caused me to apply for disability benefits. I do not recommend this firm to anyone who has similar issues to mine.

Absolutely horrible. Matthew Harmoning was so rude over the phone. I had set up an appointment and speaking with administration said I have a piece of paper that I would like to show in person. I was told that most appointments were done over the phone and it wasn't necessary. It was also talked about me not missing work to come in. So I agreed to a phone appointment. Then I was rudely accused of not saying anything like that over the phone, blaming me for not coming in when that's what I wanted in the first place. Then said I couldn't listen to the recording. I would give zero stars if possible.

Worst experience I’ve had with a law firm. You can’t ever talk to an actual lawyer. A paralegal is assigned to your case. Keri Dennis was my case manager. I spoke with her a total of 2 times in 6 weeks because I had a few questions about the process. She audibly sighed for every question I asked and almost hung up on me during both conversations because she had more important things to do than answer my questions about the disability process and my case. Horrible experience.

Then you responded to my complaint by saying other people had good experiences? And I can call and talk to the rude person that tries to hang up on me during our conversations if I have concerns? How is that helpful?

Just demands money, will not answer simple questions. Aaron specifically was unprofessional. Did not offer to find someone to answer any questions. Was not asking for legal advice, just had questions.

Horrible. If you are thinking about going with them, don't! Our lawyer was even Robert Hogland himself. We barely had communication with him and when we did he was very rushed. The communication from his staff was by far the worst. Everything with our case was changing all the time. We had no idea what was going on and we were told multiple different things. I'm not even sure what we paid for because I felt like I was always the one calling and having to do the follow up work. I absolutely hated our entire experience. Save yourself and go somewhere else.

Was in a car accident and had Meshbesher. I had no idea how to handle this situation after what happened to me. Sadly I felt the lawyer I had didn't help me enough to have my back in everything and to this day years later I still have problems from the accident. It seems like it was a case where it wasn't worth the time to help me.
They only help if they can find money in your case; other than that you are dead to them.

I wish I could say great things like other reviews. Genuinely I felt hopeful with the first call. I was treated well, brought in with open arms.

Sadly it just went down from there. In the year I was with them, I was the only one making the main calls to check up on my claim. My case manager barely explained things and told me "You were the one that came to us late". I did my best to keep my cool but I felt like I was just being treated as another number. I called the service quits, and despite my letter stating I was unhappy with the service, I'm still going to be charged if I ever get approved for my disability claim.

Surely other people will have better experiences, but this was mine.

Outcome was great — disability approved. But I was never once offered a chance to meet anyone in person. Whenever I called the receptionists, who were very polite, they would never connect me to anyone I asked for. I would leave messages that were not returned sometimes for several days. I did not know who was representing me on the final hearing until 1–2 weeks before. I always felt like a number, not a person. When you hire an attorney to help you with something you can't do yourself, you want to feel connected to them. Hoglund makes you feel totally disconnected.

I am a former client, regardless of their response. Schwebel Goetz & Sieben (SGS): be prepared, this process is about money, not justice or principle.

Over two years ago, tragedy struck our family. Our 20-year-old son died, and our wrongful death claim involved the bar he visited that night. In our first meeting with our SGS attorney, we stressed that our priority wasn’t money—it was justice. He said he understood, would file charges, and would be willing to go to court. Their investigator gathered evidence supporting our claim. Then months of near silence passed. Eventually, our attorney said he would file charges; he never did. He addressed me by my deceased son’s name, twice.

Over the following months, I personally uncovered two more critical pieces of evidence. That I had to do this myself was a major red flag—this was their job. With this new evidence, our attorney set up early mediation without our approval and before filing charges. Reluctantly, we agreed. Before the mediation, he oddly showed doubt about our claim, then shook my hand and said, “You’ve done more than my investigators.”

Mediation failed. The mediator, hand-picked by SGS, was arrogant and dismissive. Our attorney repeated that the next step was to file charges. Again, it didn’t happen. Later, the insurance company made another offer based on the mediator’s suggestion. We rejected it—too low and we still wanted charges filed. But our attorney insisted we accept, otherwise find a different attorney. He gave no specifics as to why, just vague comments about the evidence.

We never requested early mediation. We were pushed into accepting a settlement we didn’t believe in. Three times we were promised that charges would be filed—they never were. The bar avoided court, responsibility, and bad publicity. For these reasons, they won, not us.

Our attorney knew the opposing counsel, had worked with him before, and likely will again. This was a business negotiation, not representation. Once you hire an SGS lawyer, you give up control. Your goals become secondary to their financial incentives. We settled, but justice wasn’t served and our son deserved better.

Cody Scharph is fantastic! Did what she was asked, no attitude, in a very timely manner, and got me a settlement in less than the time it was supposed to take. She took the time to answer my questions as the case was progressing and is extremely friendly and likeable. Number one at the top as far as I'm concerned.

Peter Riley and his secretary and assistant are awesome. I was at my very bottom and my world had turned upside down. They assisted me with my case and got me all the help to recover from my accident. I highly recommend using them.
Kojo was very pleasant to work with, and always helped me with all my questions.
I was involved in an auto accident with my wife. I can be difficult to deal with, but Shannon Carey handled it with grace and patience. In the end I received a fair settlement.

I am giving this review 4 stars because things started off a little shaky. Overall it was a great experience. Robert Hoglund explained everything to me so I knew exactly what to expect from beginning to end. I would highly recommend this firm.
